self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[req_index] = request.num_computed_tokens
        self.block_table.add_row(request.block_ids, req_index)

        sampling_params = request.sampling_params
        assert sampling_params is not None, "pooling requests not supported yet"
        if sampling_params.sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
            # Avoid later division by zero.
            self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = -1.0
            self.greedy_reqs.add(req_id)
        else:
            self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
            self.random_reqs.add(req_id)

        self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
        if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
            self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
        top_k = sampling_params.top_k
        if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
            self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
        else:
            top_k = self.vocab_size
        self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
        self.min_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.min_p
        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
            req_index] = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
        if sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
            self.min_p_reqs.add(req_id)
        if sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0:
            self.frequency_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
        self.presence_penalties_cpu[
            req_index] = sampling_params.presence_penalty
        if sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0:
            self.presence_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
            req_index] = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
        if sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0:
            self.repetition_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
        if sampling_params.min_tokens:
            self.min_tokens[req_index] = (sampling_params.min_tokens,
                                          sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids)

        # NOTE(woosuk): self.generators should not include the requests that
        # do not have their own generator.
        if request.generator is not None:
            self.generators[req_index] = request.generator

        if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:
            self.num_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.logprobs
        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
            self.num_prompt_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
        if sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
            self.logit_bias[req_index] = sampling_params.logit_bias

        if sampling_params.allowed_token_ids:
            self.has_allowed_token_ids.add(req_id)
            if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is None:
                # Lazy allocation for this tensor, which can be large.
                # False means we don't fill with -inf.
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask = torch.zeros(self.max_num_reqs,
                                                          self.vocab_size,
                                                          dtype=torch.bool,
                                                          device=self.device)
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
                    self.max_num_reqs,
                    self.vocab_size,
                    dtype=torch.bool,
                    device="cpu")
            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index] = True
            # False means we don't fill with -inf.
            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index][
                sampling_params.allowed_token_ids] = False

        if sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids:
            self.bad_words_token_ids[
                req_index] = sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids

        # Add request lora ID
        if request.lora_request:
            lora_id = request.lora_request.lora_int_id

"""This method must always be followed by a call to condense()."""

        req_index = self.req_id_to_index.pop(req_id, None)
        if req_index is None:
            return None
        self._req_ids[req_index] = None
        self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = None

        self.greedy_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.random_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.top_p_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.top_k_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.min_p_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.min_tokens.pop(req_index, None)
        self.frequency_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.presence_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.repetition_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.generators.pop(req_index, None)
        self.num_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
        self.num_prompt_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu.pop(req_id, None)

        # LoRA
        lora_id = self.request_lora_mapping[req_index]
        if lora_id != 0:
            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].discard(req_id)
            if len(self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id]) == 0:
                self.lora_id_to_request_ids.pop(lora_id)
                self.lora_id_to_lora_request.pop(lora_id)
            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0

        self.logit_bias[req_index] = None
        self.has_allowed_token_ids.discard(req_id)
        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
            # False means we don't fill with -inf.
            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index].fill_(False)
        self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(req_index, None)
        return req_index

    def swap_states(self, i1: int, i2: int) -> None:
        old_id_i1 = self._req_ids[i1]
        old_id_i2 = self._req_ids[i2]
        self._req_ids[i1], self._req_ids[i2] =\
            self._req_ids[i2], self._req_ids[i1] # noqa
        self.req_output_token_ids[i1], self.req_output_token_ids[i2] =\
            self.req_output_token_ids[i2], self.req_output_token_ids[i1]
        assert old_id_i1 is not None and old_id_i2 is not None
        self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2] =\
            self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1]
        self.num_tokens[i1], self.num_tokens[i2] =\
            self.num_tokens[i2], self.num_tokens[i1]
        self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2] =\
            self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1]
        self.num_prompt_tokens[i1], self.num_prompt_tokens[i2] =\
            self.num_prompt_tokens[i2], self.num_prompt_tokens[i1]
        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2] =\
            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1]
        self.temperature_cpu[i1], self.temperature_cpu[i2] =\
            self.temperature_cpu[i2], self.temperature_cpu[i1]
        self.top_p_cpu[i1], self.top_p_cpu[i2] =\
            self.top_p_cpu[i2], self.top_p_cpu[i1]
        self.top_k_cpu[i1], self.top_k_cpu[i2] =\
            self.top_k_cpu[i2], self.top_k_cpu[i1]
        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1]
        self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
            self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1]
        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1]
        self.min_p_cpu[i1], self.min_p_cpu[i2] =\
            self.min_p_cpu[i2], self.min_p_cpu[i1]

        # NOTE: the following is unsafe
        # self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], =\
        #     self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...]
        # instead, we need to temporiarily copy the data for one of the indices
        # TODO(lucas): optimize this by only copying valid indices
        tmp = self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...].copy()
        self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...] = self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...]
        self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...] = tmp

        swap_dict_values(self.generators, i1, i2)
        swap_dict_values(self.min_tokens, i1, i2)
        swap_dict_values(self.bad_words_token_ids, i1, i2)

        self.request_lora_mapping[i1], self.request_lora_mapping[i2] =\
            self.request_lora_mapping[i2], self.request_lora_mapping[i1]
        self.logit_bias[i1], self.logit_bias[i2] =\
            self.logit_bias[i2], self.logit_bias[i1]

        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1], \
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2] =\
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2], \
                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1]
        self.block_table.swap_row(i1, i2)

    def condense(self, empty_req_indices: list[int]) -> None:
        """Move non-empty requests down into lower, empty indices.
        
        Args:
          empty_req_indices: empty batch indices, sorted descending.
        """
        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
        if num_reqs == 0:
            # The batched states are empty.
            self._req_ids.clear()
            self.req_output_token_ids.clear()
            return

        # NOTE(woosuk): This function assumes that the empty_req_indices
        # is sorted in descending order.
        last_req_index = num_reqs + len(empty_req_indices) - 1
        while empty_req_indices:
            # Find the largest non-empty index.
            while last_req_index in empty_req_indices:
                last_req_index -= 1

            # Find the smallest empty index.
            empty_index = empty_req_indices.pop()
            if empty_index >= last_req_index:
                break

            # Swap the states.
            req_id = self._req_ids[last_req_index]
            output_token_ids = self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index]
            assert req_id is not None
            self._req_ids[empty_index] = req_id
            self._req_ids[last_req_index] = None
            self.req_output_token_ids[empty_index] = output_token_ids
            self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index] = None
            self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = empty_index

            num_tokens = self.num_tokens[last_req_index]
            self.token_ids_cpu[empty_index, :num_tokens] = self.token_ids_cpu[
                last_req_index, :num_tokens]
            self.num_tokens[empty_index] = num_tokens
            self.num_tokens_no_spec[empty_index] = self.num_tokens_no_spec[
                last_req_index]
            self.num_prompt_tokens[empty_index] = self.num_prompt_tokens[
                last_req_index]
            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.block_table.move_row(last_req_index, empty_index)
            self.temperature_cpu[empty_index] = self.temperature_cpu[
                last_req_index]
            self.top_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_p_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.top_k_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_k_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.frequency_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.presence_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.repetition_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.min_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.min_p_cpu[last_req_index]
            generator = self.generators.pop(last_req_index, None)
            if generator is not None:
                self.generators[empty_index] = generator

            min_token = self.min_tokens.pop(last_req_index, None)
            if min_token is not None:
                self.min_tokens[empty_index] = min_token

            self.request_lora_mapping[empty_index] = self.request_lora_mapping[
                last_req_index]

            self.logit_bias[empty_index] = self.logit_bias[last_req_index]

            if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
                    empty_index] = self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
                        last_req_index]

            bad_words_token_ids = self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(
                last_req_index, None)
            if bad_words_token_ids is not None:
                self.bad_words_token_ids[empty_index] = bad_words_token_ids
            # Decrement last_req_index since it is now empty.
            last_req_index -= 1

   - Add docstring entry in class docstring (after min_p description, lines 154-156)
   - Add to `from_optional()` signature and body (after line 261, 304)
   - Add validation in `_verify_args()` (after line 428)
   - Add `self.top_a = 0.0` in greedy sampling reset (after line 381)

   - Add `top_as: list[float] = []` in `from_sampling_metadata()` (after line 399)
   - Add `do_top_a = False` flag

Now I need to thread the `top_a` values through the sampling loop, extracting them from each sampling parameter, tracking whether any are non-zero, and accumulating them for both prefill and decode phases before passing them to the tensor construction and returning the flag.

Next I'm integrating this into the sampler layer itself—unpacking the flag and tensor from the metadata, storing it as instance state, then applying the top-a filtering in the forward pass right after the min-p step, and adding the helper function to perform the actual filtering logic. Still writing TPU batch handling...

Now I need to update the OpenAI protocol files to add top_a support across ChatCompletionRequest, CompletionRequest, and potentially TranscriptionRequest, including default values and resolution logic for each.

Then I'll pass top_a through to SamplingParams in the to_sampling_params() method, add it to the available_params list in config.py, and update the spec decode utilities to recognize top_a as an unsupported parameter.

For the sampler ordering, top_a should fit into the pipeline after top-k and top-p filtering but I need to determine whether it applies before or after min_p based on how these filters interact. I'm realizing top_a and min_p are mathematically equivalent—both filter tokens based on a relative threshold against the maximum probability—though they're typically used with different value ranges in practice. Since they're the same operation, I'll implement top_a using the same approach as min_p but need to be careful about in-place operations to avoid conflicts when both are applied.

input.content:
# Plan: Add Top-A Sampling to vLLM

## Context

Top-A is a token-filtering strategy that keeps only tokens whose probability is at
least `top_a * max_probability`, then renormalises. It is mathematically identical
in form to `min_p` (both are a relative-probability threshold against the top token).
Adding it means threaded it through exactly the same set of files as `min_p` was,
with `top_a` as the new parameter name, default `0.0` (disabled), valid range
`[0.0, 1.0]`.

All paths that carry `min_p` must carry `top_a`:
- V0 sampler (CPU/GPU) – `SamplingTensors` + `sampler.py`
- V1 GPU sampler – `LogitsProcessorManager` / `MinPLogitsProcessor` pattern
- V1 TPU sampler – `InputBatch` / `TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata` / `TPUSampler`
- OpenAI-compatible entrypoints – `protocol.py` request models
- Spec-decode fast-path guard – `v1/spec_decode/utils.py`
- Generation-config override allow-list – `config.py`

---

## Step-by-step implementation

### 1 — `vllm/sampling_params.py`

**a) Class docstring** (after the `min_p:` bullet, ~line 156):
```
        top_a: Float that represents a threshold for top-a sampling. Only tokens
            whose probability is at least ``top_a`` times the probability of the
            most likely token are kept. Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable.
```

**b) Field declaration** (after line 214, `min_p: float = 0.0`):
```python
top_a: float = 0.0
```

**c) `from_optional()` signature** (after the `min_p: float = 0.0` parameter, ~line 261):
```python
top_a: float = 0.0,
```
And in the `SamplingParams(...)` constructor call (after `min_p=min_p,` ~line 304):
```python
top_a=top_a,
```

**d) Greedy-sampling reset** in `__post_init__` (after `self.min_p = 0.0` at line 381):
```python
self.top_a = 0.0
```

**e) Validation** in `_verify_args()` (after the `min_p` check, after line 428):
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("top_a must be in [0, 1], got "
                     f"{self.top_a}.")
```

**f) `__repr__`** (after `f"min_p={self.min_p}, "` at line 572):
```python
f"top_a={self.top_a}, "
```

---

### 2 — `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**a) `SamplingTensors` dataclass field** (after `min_ps: torch.Tensor` at line 379):
```python
top_as: torch.Tensor
```

**b) `from_sampling_metadata()` — variable initialisation** (after `min_ps: list[float] = []` ~line 399):
```python
top_as: list[float] = []
```
And flag (after `do_min_p = False` ~line 405):
```python
do_top_a = False
```

**c) Per-sequence extraction** in the loop (after `min_p = sampling_params.min_p` ~line 416):
```python
top_a = sampling_params.top_a
```
Flag update (after `if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS: do_min_p = True` ~line 430):
```python
if not do_top_a and top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS:
    do_top_a = True
```
Append for prompt-logprob rows (after `min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len` ~line 446):
```python
top_as += [top_a] * prefill_len
```
Append for sample rows (after `min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens` ~line 457):
```python
top_as += [top_a] * sample_lens
```

**d) `from_lists()` call** — add `top_as` as a new argument (after `min_ps,` in the call at ~line 484):
```python
top_as,
```
And extend the return tuple (change `do_min_p` → both flags, line ~495):
```python
return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p, do_top_a)
```

**e) `from_lists()` signature** — add parameter (after `min_ps: list[float],` ~line 503):
```python
top_as: list[float],
```
Create tensor (after `min_ps_t = torch.tensor(...)` block ~line 556):
```python
top_as_t = torch.tensor(

```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Top-A sampling: keep only tokens whose probability is at least
    top_a * max_probability, then let the caller renormalise via softmax.
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### 4 — `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`

Add a `TopALogitsProcessor` class (modelled exactly on `MinPLogitsProcessor`,
lines 225-306). The `apply()` method is identical in structure to `MinPLogitsProcessor.apply()`:
```python
class TopALogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.top_a_count: int = 0
        self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.top_a_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
                                                      device=device)
        self.top_a: torch.Tensor = self.top_a_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        return True

    def get_top_a_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
        return float(self.top_a_cpu[index])

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        # mirror of MinPLogitsProcessor.update_state, using top_a fields
        ...

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.top_a_count:
            return logits
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        adjusted_top_a = max_probabilities.mul_(self.top_a)
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_top_a
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

tool_use_result.content:
# Plan: Add Top-A Sampling to vLLM

## Context

Top-A is a token-filtering strategy that keeps only tokens whose probability is at
least `top_a * max_probability`, then renormalises. It is mathematically identical
in form to `min_p` (both are a relative-probability threshold against the top token).
Adding it means threaded it through exactly the same set of files as `min_p` was,
with `top_a` as the new parameter name, default `0.0` (disabled), valid range
`[0.0, 1.0]`.

All paths that carry `min_p` must carry `top_a`:
- V0 sampler (CPU/GPU) – `SamplingTensors` + `sampler.py`
- V1 GPU sampler – `LogitsProcessorManager` / `MinPLogitsProcessor` pattern
- V1 TPU sampler – `InputBatch` / `TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata` / `TPUSampler`
- OpenAI-compatible entrypoints – `protocol.py` request models
- Spec-decode fast-path guard – `v1/spec_decode/utils.py`
- Generation-config override allow-list – `config.py`

---

## Step-by-step implementation

### 1 — `vllm/sampling_params.py`

            most likely token are kept. Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable.

3. **SamplingTensors** – call `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` with a
   batch containing `top_a > 0`; verify the returned `do_top_a` flag is `True`
   and `sampling_tensors.top_as` contains the expected values.

4. **API layer** – POST a chat-completion request with `{"top_a": 0.3}` and
   confirm the parameter reaches `SamplingParams` without error.

5. **V1 logits processor** – call `TopALogitsProcessor.update_state()` with a
   batch update adding a request with `top_a=0.2`, then call `apply()` and
   verify the masking behaviour.

# Plan: Add Top-A Sampling to vLLM

## Context

Top-A is a token-filtering strategy that keeps only tokens whose probability is at
least `top_a * max_probability`, then renormalises. It is mathematically identical
in form to `min_p` (both are a relative-probability threshold against the top token).
Adding it means threaded it through exactly the same set of files as `min_p` was,
with `top_a` as the new parameter name, default `0.0` (disabled), valid range
`[0.0, 1.0]`.

All paths that carry `min_p` must carry `top_a`:
- V0 sampler (CPU/GPU) – `SamplingTensors` + `sampler.py`
- V1 GPU sampler – `LogitsProcessorManager` / `MinPLogitsProcessor` pattern
- V1 TPU sampler – `InputBatch` / `TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata` / `TPUSampler`
- OpenAI-compatible entrypoints – `protocol.py` request models
- Spec-decode fast-path guard – `v1/spec_decode/utils.py`
- Generation-config override allow-list – `config.py`

---

## Step-by-step implementation

### 1 — `vllm/sampling_params.py`

**a) Class docstring** (after the `min_p:` bullet, ~line 156):
```
        top_a: Float that represents a threshold for top-a sampling. Only tokens
            whose probability is at least ``top_a`` times the probability of the
            most likely token are kept. Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable.
```

**b) Field declaration** (after line 214, `min_p: float = 0.0`):
```python
top_a: float = 0.0
```

**c) `from_optional()` signature** (after the `min_p: float = 0.0` parameter, ~line 261):
```python

```
And in the `SamplingParams(...)` constructor call (after `min_p=min_p,` ~line 304):
```python
top_a=top_a,
```

**d) Greedy-sampling reset** in `__post_init__` (after `self.min_p = 0.0` at line 381):
```python
self.top_a = 0.0
```

**e) Validation** in `_verify_args()` (after the `min_p` check, after line 428):
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("top_a must be in [0, 1], got "
                     f"{self.top_a}.")
```

**f) `__repr__`** (after `f"min_p={self.min_p}, "` at line 572):
```python
f"top_a={self.top_a}, "
```

---

### 2 — `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**a) `SamplingTensors` dataclass field** (after `min_ps: torch.Tensor` at line 379):
```python
top_as: torch.Tensor
```

**b) `from_sampling_metadata()` — variable initialisation** (after `min_ps: list[float] = []` ~line 399):
```python

```
And flag (after `do_min_p = False` ~line 405):
```python

```

**c) Per-sequence extraction** in the loop (after `min_p = sampling_params.min_p` ~line 416):
```python

```
Flag update (after `if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS: do_min_p = True` ~line 430):
```python
if not do_top_a and top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS:
    do_top_a = True
```
Append for prompt-logprob rows (after `min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len` ~line 446):
```python

```
Append for sample rows (after `min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens` ~line 457):
```python

```

**d) `from_lists()` call** — add `top_as` as a new argument (after `min_ps,` in the call at ~line 484):
```python
top_as,
```
And extend the return tuple (change `do_min_p` → both flags, line ~495):
```python

```

**e) `from_lists()` signature** — add parameter (after `min_ps: list[float],` ~line 503):
```python
top_as: list[float],
```
Create tensor (after `min_ps_t = torch.tensor(...)` block ~line 556):
```python

    top_as,
    device="cpu",
    dtype=dtype,
    pin_memory=pin_memory,
)
```
Include in `cls(...)` constructor (after `min_ps=min_ps_t.to(...)` ~line 588):
```python

```

---

### 3 — `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

**a) `_init_sampling_tensors()`** — unpack fifth return value and store (around lines 216-223):
```python
(sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
 do_min_p, do_top_a) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(...)

self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
self._do_penalties = do_penalties
self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
self._do_min_p = do_min_p
self._do_top_a = do_top_a
```

**b) `forward()`** — read flag (after `do_min_p = self._do_min_p` ~line 267):
```python
do_top_a = self._do_top_a
```
Apply filter (after `if do_min_p: logits = _apply_min_p(...)` at line 289):
```python
if do_top_a:
    logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
```

**c) New function** `_apply_top_a` (add immediately after `_apply_min_p`, after line 441):
```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Top-A sampling: keep only tokens whose probability is at least
    top_a * max_probability, then let the caller renormalise via softmax.
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### 4 — `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`

Add a `TopALogitsProcessor` class (modelled exactly on `MinPLogitsProcessor`,
lines 225-306). The `apply()` method is identical in structure to `MinPLogitsProcessor.apply()`:
```python
class TopALogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.top_a_count: int = 0
        self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.top_a_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
                                                      device=device)
        self.top_a: torch.Tensor = self.top_a_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        return True

    def get_top_a_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
        return float(self.top_a_cpu[index])

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        # mirror of MinPLogitsProcessor.update_state, using top_a fields
        ...

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.top_a_count:
            return logits
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        adjusted_top_a = max_probabilities.mul_(self.top_a)
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_top_a
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

Register in `init_builtin_logitsprocs()` (after `min_p_logitproc`):
```python
top_a_logitproc = TopALogitsProcessor(
    pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
    device=device,
    max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
```
Add to `argmax_invariant` list:
```python
argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc, top_a_logitproc],
```

---

### 5 — `vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py`

**a) `__init__`** — add four new fields (after the `min_p_reqs` block, after line 115):
```python
self.top_a = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ), dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ), dtype=torch.float32,
                                    device="cpu", pin_memory=pin_memory)
self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
self.top_a_reqs: set[str] = set()
```

**b) `add_request()`** (after the `min_p` lines ~252-256):
```python
self.top_a_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_a
if sampling_params.top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS:
    self.top_a_reqs.add(req_id)
```

**c) `remove_request()`** (after `self.min_p_reqs.discard(req_id)` ~line 332):
```python
self.top_a_reqs.discard(req_id)
```

**d) `swap_states()`** (after the `min_p_cpu` swap ~line 389-390):
```python
self.top_a_cpu[i1], self.top_a_cpu[i2] = \
    self.top_a_cpu[i2], self.top_a_cpu[i1]
```

**e) `condense()`** (after `self.min_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.min_p_cpu[last_req_index]` ~line 474):
```python
self.top_a_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_a_cpu[last_req_index]
```

---

### 6 — `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py`

**a) `DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS`** (after `"min_p": 0.0,`):
```python
"top_a": 0.0,
```

**b) `TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata`** dataclass field (after `min_p: torch.Tensor = None`):
```python
top_a: torch.Tensor = None
```

**c) `from_input_batch()`** — fill padding (after the `min_p` fill_slice call):
```python
fill_slice(input_batch.top_a_cpu_tensor, DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_a"])
```
Return (after `min_p=...`):
```python
top_a=input_batch.top_a_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(xla_device),
```

---

### 7 — `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py`

**a) `forward()`** — add call (after `apply_min_p` call, ~line 64):
```python
if sampling_metadata.top_a is not None:
    logits = self.apply_top_a(logits, sampling_metadata.top_a)
```

**b) New method** `apply_top_a` (after `apply_min_p`, after line 145):
```python
def apply_top_a(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    adjusted_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
    valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_top_a
    logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### 8 — `vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

Three request models need the same treatment:

**`ChatCompletionRequest`** (after `min_p: Optional[float] = None` ~line 262):
```python
top_a: Optional[float] = None
```
`_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS` (after `"min_p": 0.0,` ~line 431):
```python
"top_a": 0.0,
```
`to_sampling_params()` resolution (after `min_p` block ~line 476):
```python

    top_a = default_sampling_params.get(
        "top_a", self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_a"])
```
`SamplingParams.from_optional()` call (after `min_p=min_p,` ~line 521):
```python
top_a=top_a,
```

**`CompletionRequest`** — same four changes, same locations relative to `min_p`.

**`TranscriptionRequest`** (if it also has `min_p`) — same four changes.

---

In `is_spec_decode_unsupported()` (after `or sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS` ~line 14):

```

---

### 10 — `vllm/config.py`

In `available_params` list (~line 1375, after `"min_p",`):
```python
"top_a",
```

---

## Critical files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `vllm/sampling_params.py` | Field declaration, validation, repr, from_optional |
| `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | SamplingTensors dataclass + tensor construction |

| `vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py` | Spec-decode compatibility guard |
| `vllm/config.py` | Generation-config override allow-list |

---

## Verification

1. **Unit test** – import `SamplingParams` and verify:
   - `SamplingParams(top_a=0.5)` succeeds; `top_a=1.5` raises `ValueError`
   - Greedy sampling (`temperature=0`) resets `top_a` to `0.0`

**c) New function** `_apply_top_a` (add immediately after `_apply_min_p`, after line 441):
```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Top-A sampling: keep only tokens whose probability is at least
    top_a * max_probability, then let the caller renormalise via softmax.
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

### 4 — `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`

Add a `TopALogitsProcessor` class (modelled exactly on `MinPLogitsProcessor`,
lines 225-306). The `apply()` method is identical in structure to `MinPLogitsProcessor.apply()`:
```python
class TopALogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.top_a_count: int = 0
        self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.top_a_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
                                                      device=device)
        self.top_a: torch.Tensor = self.top_a_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        return True

    def get_top_a_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
        return float(self.top_a_cpu[index])

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        # mirror of MinPLogitsProcessor.update_state, using top_a fields
        ...

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.top_a_count:
            return logits
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        adjusted_top_a = max_probabilities.mul_(self.top_a)
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_top_a
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

Register in `init_builtin_logitsprocs()` (after `min_p_logitproc`):

```

```

**b) New method** `apply_top_a` (after `apply_min_p`, after line 145):
```python
def apply_top_a(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    adjusted_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
    valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_top_a
    logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

## Critical files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `vllm/sampling_params.py` | Field declaration, validation, repr, from_optional |
| `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | SamplingTensors dataclass + tensor construction |
| `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | V0 filter function + forward() wiring |
| `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | V1 GPU TopALogitsProcessor + registration |
| `vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py` | TPU persistent-batch tensor bookkeeping |
| `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` | TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata field |
| `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` | TPU apply_top_a method + forward() wiring |
| `vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | API request model fields + to_sampling_params |
| `vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py` | Spec-decode compatibility guard |
| `vllm/config.py` | Generation-config override allow-list |

---

## Verification

1. **Unit test** – import `SamplingParams` and verify:
   - `SamplingParams(top_a=0.5)` succeeds; `top_a=1.5` raises `ValueError`
   - Greedy sampling (`temperature=0`) resets `top_a` to `0.0`

2. **V0 sampler filter** – construct a small logits tensor and call `_apply_top_a`
   directly; confirm tokens below `top_a * max_prob` are set to `-inf`.

3. **SamplingTensors** – call `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` with a
   batch containing `top_a > 0`; verify the returned `do_top_a` flag is `True`
   and `sampling_tensors.top_as` contains the expected values.

4. **API layer** – POST a chat-completion request with `{"top_a": 0.3}` and
   confirm the parameter reaches `SamplingParams` without error.

5. **V1 logits processor** – call `TopALogitsProcessor.update_state()` with a
   batch update adding a request with `top_a=0.2`, then call `apply()` and
   verify the masking behaviour.

2	
3	---
4	
5	## What Top-A is and why `min_p` is the template
6	
7	Top-A keeps tokens whose softmax probability ≥ `top_a × max_probability`. This is mathematically the same algorithm as `min_p`, just a differently-named parameter. Every file that carries `min_p` needs a parallel `top_a` change.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## Files to modify (in execution order)
12	
13	### 1 · `vllm/sampling_params.py`
14	- Add `top_a: float = 0.0` field (after `min_p`)
15	- Add docstring entry, validation (`0.0 ≤ top_a ≤ 1.0`), greedy-reset (`self.top_a = 0.0`), `from_optional()` parameter + pass-through, and `__repr__` entry
16	
17	### 2 · `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

23	- Store `self._do_top_a`, read it in `forward()`, call new `_apply_top_a()` after `_apply_min_p()`

26	### 4 · `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` (V1 GPU path)

file.content:
Here is the full implementation plan for adding `top_a` to vLLM, across all **10 files**:

---

## What Top-A is and why `min_p` is the template

Top-A keeps tokens whose softmax probability ≥ `top_a × max_probability`. This is mathematically the same algorithm as `min_p`, just a differently-named parameter. Every file that carries `min_p` needs a parallel `top_a` change.

---

## Files to modify (in execution order)

### 1 · `vllm/sampling_params.py`
- Add `top_a: float = 0.0` field (after `min_p`)
- Add docstring entry, validation (`0.0 ≤ top_a ≤ 1.0`), greedy-reset (`self.top_a = 0.0`), `from_optional()` parameter + pass-through, and `__repr__` entry

### 2 · `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`
- Add `top_as: torch.Tensor` to the `SamplingTensors` dataclass
- Extract `top_a` per sequence in `from_sampling_metadata()`, build `do_top_a` flag, accumulate into list, pass to `from_lists()`, create pinned CPU tensor → device tensor, extend return tuple to 5 elements

### 3 · `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (V0 GPU/CPU path)
- Unpack 5th value (`do_top_a`) from `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()`
- Store `self._do_top_a`, read it in `forward()`, call new `_apply_top_a()` after `_apply_min_p()`
- Add `_apply_top_a(logits, top_a)` function (identical structure to `_apply_min_p`)

### 4 · `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` (V1 GPU path)
- Add `TopALogitsProcessor` class (exact mirror of `MinPLogitsProcessor` with `top_a` fields)
- Register it in `init_builtin_logitsprocs()` → `argmax_invariant` list

### 5 · `vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py` (V1 TPU persistent batch)
- Add `top_a`, `top_a_cpu_tensor`, `top_a_cpu`, `top_a_reqs` fields in `__init__`
- Update `add_request()`, `remove_request()`, `swap_states()`, `condense()` — same pattern as the existing `min_p` lines

### 6 · `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` (V1 TPU sampling metadata)
- Add `"top_a": 0.0` to `DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS`
- Add `top_a: torch.Tensor = None` to `TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata`
- Fill padding and return `top_a` tensor in `from_input_batch()`

### 7 · `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` (V1 TPU sampler)
- Add `apply_top_a()` method (mirror of `apply_min_p()`)
- Call it in `forward()` when `sampling_metadata.top_a is not None`

### 8 · `vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` (API layer)
- Add `top_a: Optional[float] = None` to `ChatCompletionRequest`, `CompletionRequest`, and `TranscriptionRequest`
- Add `"top_a": 0.0` to each `_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS`
- Add resolution logic and pass `top_a=top_a` to `SamplingParams.from_optional()` in each `to_sampling_params()`

### 9 · `vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py`
- Add `or sampling_params.top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS` to `is_spec_decode_unsupported()`

### 10 · `vllm/config.py`
- Add `"top_a"` to the `available_params` list (~line 1375)

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
3	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
4	import copy
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from enum import Enum, IntEnum
7	from functools import cached_property
8	from typing import Annotated, Any, Optional, Union
9	
10	import msgspec
11	from pydantic import BaseModel
12	from typing_extensions import deprecated
13	
14	from vllm.logger import init_logger
15	from vllm.logits_process import LogitsProcessor
16	from vllm.transformers_utils.tokenizer import AnyTokenizer
17	
18	logger = init_logger(__name__)
19	
20	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
21	_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2
22	
23	
24	class SamplingType(IntEnum):
25	    GREEDY = 0
26	    RANDOM = 1
27	    RANDOM_SEED = 2
28	
29	
30	# maybe make msgspec?
31	@dataclass
32	class GuidedDecodingParams:
33	    """One of these fields will be used to build a logit processor."""
34	    json: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
35	    regex: Optional[str] = None
36	    choice: Optional[list[str]] = None
37	    grammar: Optional[str] = None
38	    json_object: Optional[bool] = None
39	    """These are other options that can be set"""
40	    backend: Optional[str] = None
41	    backend_was_auto: bool = False
42	    disable_fallback: bool = False
43	    disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
44	    disable_additional_properties: bool = False
45	    whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
46	    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None
47	
48	    @staticmethod
49	    def from_optional(
50	        json: Optional[Union[dict, BaseModel, str]] = None,
51	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
52	        choice: Optional[list[str]] = None,
53	        grammar: Optional[str] = None,
54	        json_object: Optional[bool] = None,
55	        backend: Optional[str] = None,
56	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
57	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
58	    ) -> Optional["GuidedDecodingParams"]:
59	        if all(arg is None for arg in (json, regex, choice, grammar,
60	                                       json_object, structural_tag)):
61	            return None
62	        # Extract json schemas from pydantic models
63	        if isinstance(json, (BaseModel, type(BaseModel))):
64	            json = json.model_json_schema()
65	        return GuidedDecodingParams(
66	            json=json,
67	            regex=regex,
68	            choice=choice,
69	            grammar=grammar,
70	            json_object=json_object,
71	            backend=backend,
72	            whitespace_pattern=whitespace_pattern,
73	            structural_tag=structural_tag,
74	        )
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self):
77	        """Validate that some fields are mutually exclusive."""
78	        guide_count = sum([
79	            self.json is not None, self.regex is not None, self.choice
80	            is not None, self.grammar is not None, self.json_object is not None
81	        ])
82	        if guide_count > 1:
83	            raise ValueError(
84	                "You can only use one kind of guided decoding but multiple are "
85	                f"specified: {self.__dict__}")
86	
87	        if self.backend is not None and ":" in self.backend:
88	            self._extract_backend_options()
89	
90	    @deprecated(
91	        "Passing guided decoding backend options inside backend in the format "
92	        "'backend:...' is deprecated. This will be removed in v0.10.0. Please "
93	        "use the dedicated arguments '--disable-fallback', "
94	        "'--disable-any-whitespace' and '--disable-additional-properties' "
95	        "instead.")
96	    def _extract_backend_options(self):
97	        """Extract backend options from the backend string."""
98	        assert isinstance(self.backend, str)
99	        self.backend, options = self.backend.split(":")
100	        options_set = set(options.strip().split(","))
101	        if "no-fallback" in options_set:
102	            self.disable_fallback = True
103	        if "disable-any-whitespace" in options_set:
104	            self.disable_any_whitespace = True
105	        if "no-additional-properties" in options_set:
106	            self.disable_additional_properties = True
107	
108	
109	class RequestOutputKind(Enum):
110	    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
111	    CUMULATIVE = 0
112	    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
113	    DELTA = 1
114	    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
115	    FINAL_ONLY = 2
116	
117	
118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
165	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
166	            can complete the sequence.
167	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
168	            output text. Defaults to False.
169	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
185	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
187	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
188	            a first argument.
189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
190	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
191	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
194	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
196	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
198	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
199	            Defaults to None.
200	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
201	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
202	            sampling implementations.
203	    """
204	
205	    n: int = 1
206	    best_of: Optional[int] = None
207	    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
208	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
209	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
210	    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
211	    temperature: float = 1.0
212	    top_p: float = 1.0
213	    top_k: int = 0
214	    min_p: float = 0.0
215	    seed: Optional[int] = None
216	    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
217	    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
218	    ignore_eos: bool = False
219	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
220	    min_tokens: int = 0
221	    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
222	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
223	    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
224	    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
225	    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
226	    detokenize: bool = True
227	    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
228	    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
229	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
230	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
231	    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
232	    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
233	    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
234	    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE
235	
236	    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
237	    # They are set in post_init.
238	    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
239	    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)
240	
241	    # Fields used to construct logits processors
242	    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
243	    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
244	    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
245	    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
246	
247	    # Fields used for bad words
248	    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
249	    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None
250	
251	    @staticmethod
252	    def from_optional(
253	        n: Optional[int] = 1,
254	        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
255	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
256	        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
257	        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
258	        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
259	        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
260	        top_k: int = 0,
261	        min_p: float = 0.0,
262	        seed: Optional[int] = None,
263	        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
264	        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
265	        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
266	        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
267	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
268	        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
269	        min_tokens: int = 0,
270	        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
271	        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
272	        detokenize: bool = True,
273	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
274	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
275	        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
276	        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
277	                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
278	        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
279	        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
280	        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
281	        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
282	        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
283	    ) -> "SamplingParams":
284	        if logit_bias is not None:
285	            # Convert token_id to integer
286	            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
287	            logit_bias = {
288	                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
289	                for token, bias in logit_bias.items()
290	            }
291	
292	        return SamplingParams(
293	            n=1 if n is None else n,
294	            best_of=best_of,
295	            presence_penalty=0.0
296	            if presence_penalty is None else presence_penalty,
297	            frequency_penalty=0.0
298	            if frequency_penalty is None else frequency_penalty,
299	            repetition_penalty=1.0
300	            if repetition_penalty is None else repetition_penalty,
301	            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
302	            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
303	            top_k=top_k,
304	            min_p=min_p,
305	            seed=seed,
306	            stop=stop,
307	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
308	            bad_words=bad_words,
309	            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
310	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
311	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
312	            min_tokens=min_tokens,
313	            logprobs=logprobs,
314	            prompt_logprobs=prompt_logprobs,
315	            detokenize=detokenize,
316	            skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens,
317	            spaces_between_special_tokens=spaces_between_special_tokens,
318	            logits_processors=logits_processors,
319	            truncate_prompt_tokens=truncate_prompt_tokens,
320	            output_kind=output_kind,
321	            guided_decoding=guided_decoding,
322	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
323	            allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids,
324	            extra_args=extra_args,
325	        )
326	
327	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
328	        # how we deal with `best_of``:
329	        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
330	        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
331	        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
332	        # when we return the result, we will check
333	        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
334	        if self.best_of:
335	            if self.best_of < self.n:
336	                raise ValueError(
337	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
338	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
339	            if not self._real_n:
340	                self._real_n = self.n
341	                self.n = self.best_of
342	
343	        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
344	            logger.warning(
345	                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
346	                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
347	                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
348	            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)
349	
350	        if self.seed == -1:
351	            self.seed = None
352	
353	        if self.stop is None:
354	            self.stop = []
355	        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
356	            self.stop = [self.stop]
357	
358	        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
359	            self.stop_token_ids = []
360	
361	        if self.bad_words is None:
362	            self.bad_words = []
363	
364	        if self.logprobs is True:
365	            self.logprobs = 1
366	
367	        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
368	            self.prompt_logprobs = 1
369	
370	        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
371	        # until sequence is finished.
372	        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
373	            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1
374	
375	        self._verify_args()
376	
377	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
378	            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
379	            self.top_p = 1.0
380	            self.top_k = 0
381	            self.min_p = 0.0
382	            self._verify_greedy_sampling()
383	
384	        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
385	        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
386	
387	    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
388	        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
389	            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
390	                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
391	        if self.n < 1:
392	            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
393	        if self.best_of is not None:
394	            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
395	                raise ValueError(
396	                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
397	            if self.best_of < 1:
398	                raise ValueError(
399	                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
400	            if self.best_of < self.n:
401	                raise ValueError(
402	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
403	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
404	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
405	            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
406	                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
407	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
408	            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
409	                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
410	        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
411	            raise ValueError(
412	                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
413	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
414	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
415	            raise ValueError(
416	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
417	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
418	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
419	        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
420	        if self.top_k < -1:
421	            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
422	                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
423	        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
424	            raise TypeError(
425	                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
426	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
427	            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
428	                             f"{self.min_p}.")
429	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
430	            raise ValueError(
431	                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
432	        if self.min_tokens < 0:
433	            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
434	                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
435	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
436	            raise ValueError(
437	                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
438	                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
439	        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
440	            raise ValueError(
441	                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
442	        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
443	            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
444	                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
445	        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
446	                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
447	            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
448	                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
449	        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
450	        if not all(isinstance(st_id, int) for st_id in self.stop_token_ids):
451	            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
452	                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
453	        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)
454	        if any(not stop_str for stop_str in self.stop):
455	            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")
456	        if self.stop and not self.detokenize:
457	            raise ValueError(
458	                "stop strings are only supported when detokenize is True. "
459	                "Set detokenize=True to use stop.")
460	        if self.best_of != self._real_n and self.output_kind == (
461	                RequestOutputKind.DELTA):
462	            raise ValueError("best_of must equal n to use output_kind=DELTA")
463	
464	    def _verify_greedy_sampling(self) -> None:
465	        if self.n > 1:
466	            raise ValueError("n must be 1 when using greedy sampling, "
467	                             f"got {self.n}.")
468	
469	    def update_from_generation_config(
470	            self,
471	            generation_config: dict[str, Any],
472	            model_eos_token_id: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
473	        """Update if there are non-default values from generation_config"""
474	
475	        if model_eos_token_id is not None:
476	            # Add the eos token id into the sampling_params to support
477	            # min_tokens processing.
478	            self._all_stop_token_ids.add(model_eos_token_id)
479	
480	        # Update eos_token_id for generation
481	        if (eos_ids := generation_config.get("eos_token_id")) is not None:
482	            # it can be either int or list of int
483	            eos_ids = {eos_ids} if isinstance(eos_ids, int) else set(eos_ids)
484	            if model_eos_token_id is not None:
485	                # We don't need to include the primary eos_token_id in
486	                # stop_token_ids since it's handled separately for stopping
487	                # purposes.
488	                eos_ids.discard(model_eos_token_id)
489	            if eos_ids:
490	                self._all_stop_token_ids.update(eos_ids)
491	                if not self.ignore_eos:
492	                    eos_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
493	                    self.stop_token_ids = list(eos_ids)
494	
495	    def update_from_tokenizer(self, tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> None:
496	        if not self.bad_words:
497	            return
498	        self._bad_words_token_ids = []
499	        for bad_word in self.bad_words:
500	            # To prohibit words both at the beginning
501	            # and in the middle of text
502	            # (related to add_prefix_space tokenizer parameter)
503	            for add_prefix_space in [False, True]:
504	                prefix = " " if add_prefix_space else ""
505	                prompt = prefix + bad_word.lstrip()
506	                prompt_token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text=prompt,
507	                                                    add_special_tokens=False)
508	
509	                # If no space at the beginning
510	                # or if prefix space produces a new word token
511	                if (not add_prefix_space) or (
512	                        add_prefix_space and prompt_token_ids[0]
513	                        != self._bad_words_token_ids[-1][0]
514	                        and len(prompt_token_ids) == len(
515	                            self._bad_words_token_ids[-1])):
516	                    self._bad_words_token_ids.append(prompt_token_ids)
517	
518	        invalid_token_ids = [
519	            token_id for bad_words_token_ids in self._bad_words_token_ids
520	            for token_id in bad_words_token_ids
521	            if token_id < 0 or token_id > tokenizer.max_token_id
522	        ]
523	        if len(invalid_token_ids) > 0:
524	            raise ValueError(
525	                f"The model vocabulary size is {tokenizer.max_token_id+1},"
526	                f" but the following tokens"
527	                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
528	                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
529	                f" 0 <= token_id <= {tokenizer.max_token_id}.")
530	
531	    @cached_property
532	    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
533	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
534	            return SamplingType.GREEDY
535	        if self.seed is not None:
536	            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
537	        return SamplingType.RANDOM
538	
539	    @property
540	    def all_stop_token_ids(self) -> set[int]:
541	        return self._all_stop_token_ids
542	
543	    @property
544	    def bad_words_token_ids(self) -> Optional[list[list[int]]]:
545	        # For internal use only. Backward compatibility not guaranteed
546	        return self._bad_words_token_ids
547	
548	    def clone(self) -> "SamplingParams":
549	        """Deep copy, but maybe not the LogitsProcessor objects.
550	
551	        LogitsProcessor objects may contain an arbitrary, nontrivial amount of
552	        data that is expensive to copy. However, if not copied, the processor
553	        needs to support parallel decoding for multiple sequences
554	        See https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/3087
555	        """
556	
557	        logit_processor_refs = None if self.logits_processors is None else {
558	            id(lp): lp.clone() if hasattr(lp, 'clone') else lp
559	            for lp in self.logits_processors
560	        }
561	        return copy.deepcopy(self, memo=logit_processor_refs)
562	
563	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
564	        return (
565	            f"SamplingParams(n={self.n}, "
566	            f"presence_penalty={self.presence_penalty}, "
567	            f"frequency_penalty={self.frequency_penalty}, "
568	            f"repetition_penalty={self.repetition_penalty}, "
569	            f"temperature={self.temperature}, "
570	            f"top_p={self.top_p}, "
571	            f"top_k={self.top_k}, "
572	            f"min_p={self.min_p}, "
573	            f"seed={self.seed}, "
574	            f"stop={self.stop}, "
575	            f"stop_token_ids={self.stop_token_ids}, "
576	            f"bad_words={self.bad_words}, "
577	            f"include_stop_str_in_output={self.include_stop_str_in_output}, "
578	            f"ignore_eos={self.ignore_eos}, "
579	            f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, "
580	            f"min_tokens={self.min_tokens}, "
581	            f"logprobs={self.logprobs}, "
582	            f"prompt_logprobs={self.prompt_logprobs}, "
583	            f"skip_special_tokens={self.skip_special_tokens}, "
584	            "spaces_between_special_tokens="
585	            f"{self.spaces_between_special_tokens}, "
586	            f"truncate_prompt_tokens={self.truncate_prompt_tokens}, "
587	            f"guided_decoding={self.guided_decoding}, "
588	            f"extra_args={self.extra_args})")
589	
590	
591	class BeamSearchParams(
592	        msgspec.Struct,
593	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
594	        # required for @cached_property.
595	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
596	    """Beam search parameters for text generation."""
597	    beam_width: int
598	    max_tokens: int
599	    ignore_eos: bool = False
600	    temperature: float = 0.0
601	    length_penalty: float = 1.0
602	    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
603
